definition: BoJ normalization is the highest-probability individual catalyst for carry disruption, following a foreshock → main event pattern. July 2006 → August 2007 (13-month gap) is the closest analogue to August 2024. Carry positions rebuilt ~60-70% per CFTC data, with surviving positions more leveraged and concentrated due to selection bias.
evidence: CFTC data showing ~60-70% position rebuilding post-August 2024
Selection bias: weakest hands exited first, residual carry more concentrated
JPY 25-35% below PPP fair value at ~148-155 (REER verifiable)
US-Japan 2-year rate differential ~350-400bp, compressing ~25-50bp per BoJ hike
August 2024 pause revealed BoJ reaction function as circuit-breaker
Scenario taxonomy: stop-go, soft normalization, external shock
Per-action unwind probability ~3-8% conditional range (pair_3 debate resolution)
open_questions: Current 19-month gap exceeds both historical precedents (13-14 months)
Per-step dislocation probability CI [2%, 35%] makes cumulative estimates uninformative
Whether JPY safe-haven property survives BoJ reaching 0.75-1.0%
Half-life of BoJ circuit-breaker credibility (does it erode with each use or strengthen)
Whether stop-go scenario leads to worse eventual outcomes than continuous normalization
Precise conditioning set for unwind probability: positioning levels, vol regime, credit cycle stage
